corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: The results show that, overall, SAC performs comparably to the baseline methods on the easier tasks and outperforms them on the harder tasks with a large margin, both in terms of learning speed and the final performance.

The quantitative results attained by SAC in our experiments also compare very favorably to results reported by other methods in prior work , indicating that both the sample efficiency and final performance of SAC on these benchmark tasks exceeds the state of the art.

The quantitative results attained by SAC in our experiments also compare very favorably to results reported by other methods in prior work , indicating that both the sample efficiency and final performance of SAC on these benchmark tasks exceeds the state of the art.
The results in the previous section suggest that algorithms based on the maximum entropy principle can outperform conventional RL methods on challenging tasks such as the humanoid tasks.

Our results suggest that stochastic, entropy maximizing reinforcement learning algorithms can provide a promising avenue for improved robustness and stability, and further exploration of maximum entropy methods, including methods that incorporate second order information (e.g., trust regions ) or more expressive policy classes is an exciting avenue for future work.
We compare our method to prior techniques on a range of challenging continuous control tasks from the OpenAI gym benchmark suite and also on the rllab implementation of the Humanoid task .

The quantitative results attained by SAC in our experiments also compare very favorably to results reported by other methods in prior work , indicating that both the sample efficiency and final performance of SAC on these benchmark tasks exceeds the state of the art.
In this paper, we propose soft actor-critic, an offpolicy actor-critic deep RL algorithm based on the maximum entropy reinforcement learning framework.
In this paper, we propose soft actor-critic, an offpolicy actor-critic deep RL algorithm based on the maximum entropy reinforcement learning framework.
In this paper, we propose soft actor-critic, an offpolicy actor-critic deep RL algorithm based on the maximum entropy reinforcement learning framework.
In this framework, the actor aims to maximize expected reward while also maximizing entropy.
Prior deep RL methods based on this framework have been formulated as Q-learning methods.

By combining off-policy updates with a stable stochastic actor-critic formulation, our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on a range of continuous control benchmark tasks, outperforming prior on-policy and off-policy methods.

By combining off-policy updates with a stable stochastic actor-critic formulation, our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on a range of continuous control benchmark tasks, outperforming prior on-policy and off-policy methods.

By combining off-policy updates with a stable stochastic actor-critic formulation, our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on a range of continuous control benchmark tasks, outperforming prior on-policy and off-policy methods.
Unfortunately, the combination of off-policy learning and high-dimensional, nonlinear function approximation with neural networks presents a major challenge for stability and convergence .
This challenge is further exacerbated in continuous state and action spaces, where a separate actor network is often used to perform the maximization in Q-learning.

A commonly used algorithm in such settings, deep deterministic policy gradient (DDPG) , provides for sample-efficient learning but is notoriously challenging to use due to its extreme brittleness and hyperparameter sensitivity .
We explore how to design an efficient and stable modelfree deep RL algorithm for continuous state and action spaces.
We explore how to design an efficient and stable modelfree deep RL algorithm for continuous state and action spaces.
To that end, we draw on the maximum entropy framework, which augments the standard maximum reward reinforcement learning objective with an entropy maximization term .

In this paper, we demonstrate that we can devise an offpolicy maximum entropy actor-critic algorithm, which we call soft actor-critic (SAC), which provides for both sampleefficient learning and stability.

This algorithm extends readily to very complex, high-dimensional tasks, such as the Humanoid benchmark with 21 action dimensions, where off-policy methods such as DDPG typically struggle to obtain good results .

This algorithm extends readily to very complex, high-dimensional tasks, such as the Humanoid benchmark with 21 action dimensions, where off-policy methods such as DDPG typically struggle to obtain good results .

We present a convergence proof for policy iteration in the maximum entropy framework, and then introduce a new algorithm based on an approximation to this procedure that can be practically implemented with deep neural networks, which we call soft actor-critic.
